covered_sentences: Pushing me forward.
As soon as we were in the sitting room, Heathcliff locked the door.
"What are you doing?" cried Catherine.
"I'm going to keep you here until you marry my son," said Heathcliff.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Three-panel layout; left panel: Heathcliff shoving Nelly forward through the doorway; center panel: Heathcliff locking the sitting room door; right panel: Catherine confronting Heathcliff as he announces his plan.

Left panel: Heathcliff (tall adult male, dark brown skin, short black slightly messy hair, dark gentleman's coat, white shirt, dark trousers, leather boots) plants one large hand firmly on Nelly's upper back, pushing her through the doorway; Nelly (dark brown hair neatly pinned up, dark housekeeper dress and white apron) stumbles forward, arms slightly raised for balance, expression alarmed. Center panel: Heathcliff stands at the heavy wooden door, key inserted, turning it with a decisive click; his broad back faces the room; Catherine (dark eyes, golden natural curly hair, light cream dress past knee) and Nelly stand behind him, Catherine's mouth open in alarm; the sound effect "CLICK" hand-drawn in bold letters near the lock. Right panel: Heathcliff turns to face Catherine with a cold, controlled expression; Catherine plants both feet apart and glares up at him, fists at her sides; a speech bubble above Catherine reads "What are you doing?!" in handwritten English; a speech bubble above Heathcliff reads "Until you marry my son" in handwritten English; red jagged anger lines drawn beside Catherine convey outrage; Linton (light golden curly hair, pale face, light gentleman's shirt and trousers) sits hunched in a wooden chair in the background, expression frightened and guilty.

Wuthering Heights sitting room: stone walls, white stone floor, large stone fireplace on the right, oak dresser on the left with tin dishes, wooden beams overhead with cured meats hanging, dark wood chairs, heavy wooden door now locked at left, side door visible at back right.

Dim indoor light, cold stone tones; no warm fire; the locked door casts a heavy psychological shadow; overall palette oppressive and grey.

Four identifiable characters: Heathcliff — dark brown skin, short black slightly messy hair, dark gentleman's coat and white shirt, tall imposing build, head exceeds door frame by half a head; Catherine — dark eyes, golden natural curly hair, light cream dress past knee, petite, head slightly below adult shoulder height; Nelly — dark brown hair neatly pinned up, round kind face, stocky medium frame, dark housekeeper dress and white apron; Linton — light golden curly hair, slender frail, pale face, light gentleman's shirt and trousers, seated in background.

covered_sentences: "I'm not afraid of you!" cried Catherine, starting to scratch and bite his hand in her attempt to get the key.
"You cannot keep me here.
My father will be worried about me.
Let me go home now.
I will marry Linton tomorrow."

covered_sentences: "Yes," said Heathcliff.
"You will marry Linton tomorrow, but you will not go home tonight.
I am very glad to hear that your father will be worried.
It makes me all the more determined to keep you here."

covered_sentences: Catherine bit his hand hard, but he grasped her arm and slapped her face several times.
Then he took us upstairs and locked us in a bedroom.
We looked for a way to escape, but the windows were too narrow.

Right panel: the upstairs imprisonment bedroom — stone walls, wooden floor, simple wooden bed, a very narrow stone-framed window on the far wall; Catherine stands on the wooden chair under the window, hands pressed against the stone frame, peering out; Nelly stands nearby, arms crossed, expression defeated; Catherine's shoulder width is clearly wider than the window opening, making escape visibly impossible; a small red "X" hand-drawn beside the window signals no exit.

Left panel: Wuthering Heights sitting room near the staircase — stone walls, stone floor, heavy locked door behind. Right panel: upstairs imprisonment bedroom — stone walls, wooden floor, simple wooden bed with plain linen, very narrow stone-framed window, wooden chair and washstand to the right, heavy wooden door with iron bolt at front right.

covered_sentences: We were imprisoned and there was nothing we could do.
The next morning Heathcliff opened the door and took Catherine away.
Then he locked the door again.
I was left alone in that room for five days.
The only person I saw in all that time was Hareton, who brought me food but said nothing.
I was left alone to wonder about what was happening to my young mistress and how my poor master would bear it when he heard all.
storyboard_assets.prompt:
Two-panel layout; left panel: Heathcliff's hand relocking the door after taking Catherine away, Nelly alone in the room; right panel: Hareton briefly appearing at the door to deliver food, then leaving without a word — Nelly isolated in the stone room.

Left panel: the upstairs imprisonment bedroom; Nelly (dark brown hair neatly pinned up, dark housekeeper dress and white apron) stands in the center of the room, arms at her sides, looking toward the heavy wooden door just as the iron bolt slides shut from outside — only Heathcliff's large hand visible around the door edge; the room is otherwise empty; Catherine is gone; a dark heavy silence is communicated by minimal detail; a small thought-cloud above Nelly contains a tiny simplified sketch of Catherine in her dress and a question mark, conveying Nelly's anxious wondering; hand-drawn tally marks on the stone wall near the bed reading five vertical strokes indicate the five days of imprisonment. Right panel: the heavy wooden door is open a crack; Hareton (teenage male, thick dark brown messy hair, rough young face, stocky broad-shouldered, dark coarse work shirt and trousers, rough hands, head slightly above adult male shoulder height) stands in the doorway holding a tray with a bowl and bread, expression blank and closed; he does not step inside; Nelly sits on the edge of the simple wooden bed, hands folded in her lap, looking up at him with a mixture of gratitude and despair; Hareton's speech bubble is empty — a bubble outline with nothing inside it, signaling he says nothing; Nelly's thought-cloud reappears with a simplified sketch of Edgar's worried face and another question mark.

Upstairs imprisonment bedroom: stone walls, wooden floor, simple wooden bed with plain linen, very narrow stone-framed window letting in minimal cold light, wooden chair and washstand to the right, heavy wooden door with iron bolt at front right.
